name: Chuck Willis
description: American blues, rhythm and blues, and rock and roll singer and songwriter (1926-1958)
abstract: Harold "Chuck" Willis (January 31, 1926 – April 10, 1958) was an American blues, rhythm and blues, and rock and roll singer and songwriter. His biggest hits, "C.

works_created.t: Hang Up My Rock and Roll Shoes

about_quotes.speaker: Ruth Brown
about_quotes.evidence: other artists like myself, — Jackie Wilson, The Drifters, The Clovers, The Flamingos, Chuck Willis, Roy Brown, Charles Brown, Wynonie Harris, to name only a few — was referred to as race music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ruth Brown interviewed by Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Tommy Ridgley
about_quotes.evidence: Chuck Willis, Chuck Willis came by, he stayed at the Dew Drop... And I remember the weekend when Chuck Willis was here... he told me, take that saxophone player

about_quotes.speaker: Lawrence Cotton
about_quotes.evidence: He's from Atlanta. He came down there with Chuck Willis. Chuck Willis' man came there.
